summary: Tested Inventory Management App (React Native Expo + AsyncStorage) via web preview at 375x667 mobile viewport. CRUD create/read/update, stats, persistence, Rupiah formatting, numeric-only inputs, low-stock color, and modal open all work correctly. Two CRITICAL web-specific defects discovered: (1) React Native Web does NOT implement Alert.alert with multiple buttons, so the Delete confirmation does NOTHING on web (item is never deleted - onPress callbacks never fire), and (2) Form validation alerts are silent on web (no dialog fires, user has no feedback when required fields are empty). Validation logic still blocks submission, but the UX is broken on web. These may work correctly on native iOS/Android.

ui_bugs.issue: React Native Web (react-native-web ^0.21.0) does not support Alert.alert with buttons. Clicking 'Hapus' button produces NO visible dialog, NO browser confirm, and NO delete occurs because the button onPress callbacks never fire. Tested: before=1 card, clicked Hapus, dialogs_fired=0, after=1 card (item NOT removed). Delete feature is effectively BROKEN on web preview.

Alert.alert('Validasi', ...) calls produce no visible message on web (dialogs_fired=0). When user clicks Simpan/Tambah with empty fields the modal correctly stays open, but the user receives NO feedback explaining why. Validation logic works (submission blocked), but messaging is invisible on web.

issues: Minor: modalFooter (save/cancel buttons) renders at y=603 on a 667px viewport leaving very little margin; Playwright flagged 'element outside viewport' for normal clicks (force/JS click succeeded). On devices with on-screen keyboard the footer could be occluded. Consider reducing form padding or ensuring scrollable footer.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warnings: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' appears on card and fab styles. Non-blocking but should migrate to boxShadow for future RN compatibility.

action_items: CRITICAL: Replace Alert.alert-based delete confirmation with a custom in-app confirmation Modal (e.g., a second Modal component with Batal/Hapus TouchableOpacity buttons) so it works on both web and native. Alternatively detect Platform.OS === 'web' and use window.confirm() which does support OK/Cancel.

HIGH: Replace form-validation Alert.alert calls with inline error text below each TextInput, or use a Toast/banner component. Inline validation is better UX on both web and native and doesn't depend on Alert implementation.
LOW: Migrate deprecated shadow* style props to boxShadow to silence RN Web 0.21 deprecation warnings.
LOW: Verify the modal footer (save/cancel) stays visible when the on-screen keyboard opens on smaller devices; KeyboardAvoidingView is present but footer sits close to the viewport bottom.
critical_code_review_comments: handleDelete uses Alert.alert with cancel/destructive buttons - this pattern is unreliable on react-native-web. Use Platform.select or a custom Modal.
validateForm surfaces errors only through Alert.alert - consider inline field errors for cross-platform parity.
Item.id is generated with Date.now()+random substring - fine for local storage but not globally unique; acceptable for this single-user app.
parseInt(item.stock, 10) runs on every render inside renderItem; minor but could be memoised. Acceptable for current list sizes.
File is 731 lines - close to the 700-line guideline. Splitting styles and the modal into separate components would improve maintainability.

success_rate.frontend: ~75% (9/12 feature areas PASS; delete confirm and validation alerts fail on web; modal UX minor issue)

seed_data_creation: During testing created items: 'Indomie Goreng' (Makanan, Rp 3.500, 20), 'Susu UHT' (Minuman, Rp 15.000, 3), edited to 'Susu UHT Edited' stock 99, 'TestLowStock' (Test, Rp 1.000, 2), 'DelTest'. These may persist in AsyncStorage/localStorage on the preview.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Testing is on https://inventory-crud-5.preview.emergentagent.com at mobile viewport 375x667. React Native Web silently drops Alert.alert with buttons - verify main agent's replacement works on both web (window.confirm or custom Modal) and native. All testIDs in the review_request are correct and functional. To trigger save-btn reliably in the modal, use JS click: `await page.evaluate(() => document.querySelector('[data-testid="save-btn"]').click())` as Playwright sometimes flags the button as outside viewport inside the sliding modal.
rca_of_issue: ROOT CAUSE: react-native-web ^0.21.0 does not implement Alert.alert with button arrays (a long-standing gap tracked at necolas/react-native-web#1026). On web, Alert.alert either no-ops or shows only the title/message via window.alert with a single OK button; either way the cancel/destructive onPress callbacks are never invoked. REPRODUCTION: (1) Open app, add any item, (2) click 'Hapus' on the card, (3) observe: no dialog is displayed and the item remains in the list. Similarly for validation: clicking Simpan with empty fields correctly blocks submission (validateForm returns false) but Alert.alert('Validasi', ...) produces no visible output. MITIGATION: Use Platform.OS === 'web' branching with window.confirm() for destructive actions and inline <Text> error components for form validation; or build a reusable custom ConfirmModal that works identically on all platforms.
